category_title: Reserved students with Spoken Language Disorders who require initial scaffolding
minimal_context: The student is participating in an interactive read-aloud of 'A Leaf Can Be' and is asked to explain how she would conceal herself during hide and seek.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are talking about the book 'A Leaf Can Be'. How did you conceal yourself when playing hide and seek?

STUDENT: I wore purple and I go hide in purple.

TUTOR: Can you use our new vocabulary word to tell me what you did?

STUDENT: (Speaks quietly) I just hide in the purple.

TUTOR: Keep trying. What is the word we are practicing?

STUDENT: (Looks down and remains quiet) I don't know.

options.A: Devoting a classroom bulletin board to read-aloud book recommendations with slips available for students.
options.B: Seating the students in a circle during the read-aloud while remaining outside the circle.
options.C: Carefully selecting 3-5 robust 'Tier Two' vocabulary words that will impact learning.
options.D: Introducing a retelling strategy with a familiar text, selecting favorite stories for students to retell.
